spans.en: Make thee an ark of gopher-wood rooms shalt thou make [in] the ark and shalt pitch [it] (from) within and (from) without with pitch
A window shalt thou make to the ark and to a cubit shalt thou finish [it] from above and the door of the ark shalt thou set in its side [with] lower second and third [stories] shalt thou make [it]
But I will establish my covenant with thee and thou shalt come into the ark thou and thy sons and thy wife and thy sons' wives
And of every living thing of all flesh two of every [kind] shalt thou bring into the ark to keep [them] alive with thee male and female shall they be
Of the fowl after its kind and of the cattle after its kind of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind two of every [kind] shall come unto thee to keep [them] alive
And … did Noah according to all that commanded him God so he did
Of every clean beast the clean thou shalt take to thee seven [by] seven male and (his) female and of the beast[s] which are not clean two male and (his) female
Also of the fowl of the heaven seven [by] seven male and female to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth
For after … more seven days I will cause to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights and I will wipe off every existing thing which I have made from (upon) the face of the earth
according to all that commanded him
six hundred year[s] old when the flood of waters was upon the earth
and his sons and his wife and the wives of his sons with him into the ark because of the waters of the flood
en_tokens: and the wives of his sons

spans.en: Of the clean beast[s] the clean and of the beast[s] which are not clean and of the fowl and all that creeps upon the ground
Two [by] two they went in unto Noah into the ark male and female as … commanded God Noah
And it came to pass after the seven days that the waters of the flood were upon the earth

In the six hundredth year of … life Noah's in the … month second on the seventeenth day of the month on this day were broken up all the fountains of the great abyss and the windows of the heaven were opened
And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights
On this very day went in Noah and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth the sons of Noah and the wife of Noah and the three wives of his sons with them into the ark
en_tokens: and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth

spans.en: They and every beast after its kind and all the cattle after their kind and every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth after its kind and every fowl after its kind every bird of every wing
into the ark two [by] two of all flesh in which [was] in it the spirit of life
And those that went in male and female of all flesh went in as … commanded him God and … shut the Lord behind him
And … was the flood forty days upon the earth and the waters increased and lifted the ark and it rose from [upon] the earth
And the waters grew mighty and increased exceedingly upon the earth and the ark went upon the face of the waters
And the waters grew exceedingly mighty upon the earth and (they) were covered all the high mountains which [were] under all the whole heaven
Fifteen cubit[s] upwards did the waters grow mighty and the mountains were covered
And … perished all flesh that creeps upon the earth (in the) fowl and (in the) cattle and (in the) beast[s] and (in) every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth and all man
All which [had] the breath of the spirit of life in his nostrils of all that [was] on the dry land died

And he wiped off every existing thing which [was] upon the face of the ground from man unto cattle unto creeping thing and unto the fowl of the heaven and they were wiped off from the earth and … only was left
And … remembered God Noah and every living [thing] and all the cattle which [were] with him in the ark and … caused … to pass God a wind over the earth and the waters sank
And … retreated the waters from (upon) the earth continually retreating and … diminished the waters [after] the end of a hundred and fifty day[s]
And … rested the ark in the … month seventh on the seventeenth day of the month upon the mountains of
And the waters were continually diminishing until the … month tenth in the tenth [month] on the first [day] of the month were seen the tops of the mountains

But … did not find the dove a resting-place for the sole of her foot and she returned unto him into the ark for waters [were] upon the face of all the earth and he put forth his hand and took her and brought her unto him into the ark
that … were abated the waters from (upon) the earth
And it came to pass in the … and first six hundredth year in the first [month] in the first [day] of the month [that] were dried up the waters from (upon) the earth and … removed
And in the … month second on the twenty seventh day of the month was … dry the earth

Every living [thing] which [is] with thee of all flesh (in the) fowl and (in the) cattle and (in) every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth bring out with thee and let them swarm in the earth and be fruitful and multiply upon the earth
and his sons and his wife and the wives of his sons with him
en_tokens: and the wives of his sons

spans.en: and he took of all (the) clean cattle clean and of all (the) clean fowl clean and he offered burnt-offerings on the altar

unto his heart I will not continue to curse again the ground for the sake of man because the purpose of the heart of man [is] evil from his youth and I will not continue again to smite every living [thing] as I have done
en_tokens: and I will not continue again

spans.en: While the earth remains seed [time] and harvest and cold and heat and summer and winter and day and night shall not cease
And … blessed God Noah and his sons and he said to them Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth

And the fear of you and the terror of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every fowl of the heaven upon every [thing] which creeps [upon] the ground and upon all the fish[es] of the sea into your hands are they delivered
Every moving thing which lives shall be to you for food as the green herb I have given to you all
But flesh with its soul [with] its blood you shall not eat
And surely your blood of your souls I will demand of the hand of every beast I will demand it and of the hand of man of the hand of a man his brother I will demand the soul of man
And … spoke God unto Noah and unto his sons with him saying
And I behold, I establish my covenant with you and with your seed after you
And with every living creature which [is] with you with the fowl with the cattle and with every beast of the earth with you of all that go out of the ark even every beast of the earth
And I will establish my covenant with you that … shall not be cut off all flesh any more by the waters of the flood and there shall not be any more a flood to destroy the earth
en_tokens: that … shall not be cut off

spans.en: And … said God This [is] the sign of the covenant which I make between me and (between) you and (between) every living creature which is with you for perpetual generations
My bow have I set in the cloud that it shall be for a sign of a covenant between me and (between) the earth
That I will remember my covenant which [is] between me and (between) you and (between) every living creature of all flesh and … shall no more become the waters (for) a flood to destroy all flesh
And when … will be the bow in the cloud then I will look upon it to remember a covenant of perpetual generations between God and (between) every living creature of all flesh which [is] upon the earth
en_tokens: then I will look upon it

spans.en: And … said God unto Noah This [is] the sign of the covenant which I have established between me and (between) all flesh which [is] upon the earth

the garment and put [it] upon the shoulder[s] of both of them and went backwards and covered the nakedness of their father and their faces [were] backwards and the nakedness of their father they did not see
from his wine and knew what [there] did to him his youngest son
And he said Blessed [be] the Lord the God of Shem and Canaan [shall be] a servant to them
… shall enlarge God (to) Japheth and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem and Canaan shall be a servant to them
From that land he went out [to] Assyria and built Nineveh and Rehoboth-Ir and Calah

spans.note: one [kind of] words or, one speech

spans.en: And they said one to another Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly and … was to them the brick for stone and (the) slime was to them for mortar
And they said Come, let us build for us a city and a tower that its top [may reach] unto the heaven and let us make for us a name lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of all the earth
Behold [they are] one people and one language [is] to all of them and this [is what] they begin to do and now shall not be restrained from them all which they plan to do
Come, let us go down and there confound their language that they may not understand one another's language
upon the face of all the earth
nineteen year[s] and a hundred year[s] and begat sons and daughters
And … took Abram and Nahor for themselves wives the name of Abram's wife [was] Sarai and the name of Nahor's wife [was] Milcah the daughter of Haran and the father of Iscah
